page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores with hyper-threading running at 3.5 GHz, supported by 256 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. The instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and software compilation using Clang. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth fall within the average range. The hardware supports advanced vector and cryptography extensions, including AVX-512 and AES-NI, which contribute to strong performance in encryption tests and ray tracing.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps network capacity, this instance is optimized for network-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks. The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe storage and high network throughput provides a resource-dense profile, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high I/O performance without the need for external storage attachments.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance configured with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it integrates 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and provides 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing capabilities, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, while single-core metrics remain average. The CPU supports AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography. With a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-speed local storage, this instance is well-suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and network-bound applications.
